summary: Microsoft documents third-party app and content suggestions with the user policy HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent\DisableThirdPartySuggestions, and Microsoft Learn also documents the user-side ContentDeliveryManager value SystemPaneSuggestionsEnabled = 0. The current app writes the documented user-side value, so this record is now validated.

setting.name: App Suggestions in Start
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can suggest promoted apps and related content in places like the Start menu. People often turn this off when they want a cleaner Start experience.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable app suggestions to reduce promotional content and make Start feel less noisy.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning suggestions off can make Start quieter, but the current product issue is that the app writes a user-side ContentDeliveryManager value while the closest official Microsoft source in this record documents a different CloudContent policy.
setting.professional_notes: CloudContent.admx documents the official user policy at HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent\DisableThirdPartySuggestions with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. Microsoft Learn's VDI optimization guidance also documents HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager\SystemPaneSuggestionsEnabled = 0 as part of the supported user-side suggestion settings. The current app writes that same user-side value.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Spotlight features may still suggest apps and content from third-party publishers.

allowed_values.label: Third-party suggestions allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and Windows is not being told to block third-party suggestions.

allowed_values.label: Third-party suggestions turned off
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Spotlight features such as suggested apps in Start will no longer suggest apps and content from third-party publishers.

allowed_values.label: No observed user value
allowed_values.meaning: No explicit ContentDeliveryManager value is present in this record for Start suggestions.

allowed_values.label: Observed app off preference
allowed_values.meaning: The current app writes 0 here, but this record does not yet publish that user path as the official Microsoft control.

targets.notes: Tracked as an observed app write only.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app targets the same feature area, but it writes a user-side ContentDeliveryManager value instead of the official CloudContent policy documented in this record.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 and Windows 11 systems exposing the CloudContent third-party suggestions policy

states.rationale: Leave the official user policy unset.
Do not treat the observed ContentDeliveryManager value as an official baseline.

windows_defaults.notes: If the official policy is not configured, Windows Spotlight features may still suggest third-party apps and content.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems where Start suggestions are not centrally managed
avoid_for: People who want a strict policy-backed block

states.rationale: Leave the official user policy unset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to keep its ordinary suggestions behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want third-party Start suggestions formally blocked by policy.
tradeoffs: Start and related surfaces may still show promoted content.
The product avoids publishing an unsourced user-side mapping as official.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app profile
intended_for: People documenting what the product currently writes
avoid_for: Anyone expecting a published official policy mapping

states.rationale: Observed user-side off preference used by the current app.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting current product behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a different Start suggestions behavior.
tradeoffs: The product targets the same experience users care about.
The current write is now documented by Microsoft Learn.
recommended_profiles.notes: The observed user-side setting is now formally sourced.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: VDI optimization guidance - ContentDeliveryManager suggestion setting
evidence.summary: Microsoft Learn documents HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager\SystemPaneSuggestionsEnabled = 0 in the VDI optimization guidance for suggested content in Settings.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CloudContent.admx DisableThirdPartySuggestions mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps DisableThirdPartySuggestions to HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CloudContent.adml DisableThirdPartySuggestions help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy stops Windows Spotlight features such as suggested apps in Start from suggesting apps and content from third-party publishers.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\ContentDeliveryManager\SystemPaneSuggestionsEnabled = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: nohuto mirror - app suggestions registry evidence
evidence.summary: The local nohuto mirror records the CloudContent policy name DisableThirdPartySuggestions and also shows the observed user-side ContentDeliveryManager setting SystemPaneSuggestionsEnabled in the registry dumps.

decision.why: Microsoft Learn documents the ContentDeliveryManager value that the app writes, and the official CloudContent policy is already documented in the same record.

validation_proof.notes: Validated against Microsoft Learn VDI optimization guidance that documents the same user-side ContentDeliveryManager suggestion value. The local nohuto mirror also captures the related CloudContent policy name and the observed registry setting in the source mirrors.
